title: muxcover refuses to create MUX4 for 3:1 mux
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

`yosys -p "proc; opt; wreduce; techmap; muxcover; stat"`

```
module mux_if_bal_3_1 #(parameter N=3, parameter W=1) (input [N*W-1:0] i, input [$clog2(N)-1:0] s, output reg [W-1:0] o);
always @* begin
    o <= {{W{{1'bx}}}};
    if (s[0] == 1'b0)
     if (s[1] == 1'b0)
      o <= i[0*W+:W];
     else
      o <= i[1*W+:W];
    else
     if (s[1] == 1'b0)
      o <= i[2*W+:W];
end
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

```
   Number of cells:                  1
     $_MUX4_                         1
```
as is the case when `wreduce` is removed (which optimises 1/3 `$_MUX_` cells away)

## Actual behavior

```
   Number of cells:                  2
     $_MUX_                          2
```
Even when changing the mux4 cost by specifying the new `-mux4=0` argument to `muxcover`.

Same behaviour (and perhaps more a more compelling case) is the same behaviour occurs for 7:1 mux should create a MUX8, and 15:1 mux should create a MUX16.

In fact, I want to influence the algorithm in such a way to always "round up": 
* \>= 3:1 mux into MUX4
* \>=5:1 mux into MUX8
* \>=9:1 mux into MUX16
which currently changing the costs does not do.
